value: Binance-affiliated entities are listed by Binance as holding registrations, licenses, authorisations, or approvals in multiple jurisdictions.

Examples include France DASP registration E2022-037, Italy DASP registration PSV5, Spain VASP registration D661, Poland VASP registration RDWW-465, Sweden registration 66822, and listed ADGM regulated entities.

final_profile.profile_summary: Binance is a global digital-asset exchange and blockchain infrastructure brand operating through multiple jurisdiction-specific entities. Its publicly described activities include digital-asset trading, custody, payments, education, research, Web3, wallet, NFT, and institutional services. No single global legal entity, headquarters, incorporation jurisdiction, or group-wide ownership structure was established from the supplied sources.

subject_risk_assessment.risk_calculation: Highest scorable matter score 80 from M001, plus 5 points for two or more independent scorable matters: 80 + 5 = 85. Confidence was not used in the arithmetic.

matters.matter_summary: Binance Holdings Limited pleaded guilty in the United States to Bank Secrecy Act and unlicensed money-transmission offenses and to violating the International Emergency Economic Powers Act. The matter was resolved through a criminal plea and monetary resolution exceeding $4 billion.

event_geography.jurisdiction_or_authority: U.S. Department of Justice; federal criminal proceedings

timeline.description: Binance Holdings Limited pleaded guilty to conspiracy to violate the Bank Secrecy Act and to fail to register as a money transmitting business, failure to register as a money transmitting business, and violating the International Emergency Economic Powers Act. Binance agreed to pay more than $4 billion.

supporting_sources.title: Binance and CEO Plead Guilty to Federal Charges in $4B Resolution
supporting_sources.supports: The confirmed guilty plea, offenses, and monetary resolution involving Binance Holdings Limited.

matters.risk_calculation: 40 typology + 40 confirmed stage + 0 direct attribution + 0 recency = 80.
matters.key_evidence: The U.S. Department of Justice reported that Binance Holdings Limited pleaded guilty and agreed to pay more than $4 billion to resolve the investigation.
matters.key_facts: The confirmed resolution involved Bank Secrecy Act violations, failure to register as a money transmitting business, and violations of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act.

supporting_sources.title: Binance and CEO Plead Guilty to Federal Charges in $4B Resolution
supporting_sources.supports: Binance Holdings Limited's guilty plea and payment exceeding $4 billion.

matters.matter_summary: The CFTC brought a civil enforcement action alleging that Binance and related entities operated an illegal digital-asset derivatives exchange and willfully evaded U.S. law. A federal court later entered an order requiring Binance to pay $2.7 billion and imposing permanent injunctive relief.

event_geography.jurisdiction_or_authority: U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission; U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Illinois

timeline.description: The CFTC filed a civil enforcement action alleging that Binance operated an illegal digital-asset derivatives exchange, acted as an unregistered futures commission merchant, and willfully evaded U.S. law.

supporting_sources.title: CFTC Charges Binance and Its Founder, Changpeng Zhao, with Willful Evasion of Federal Law and Operating an Illegal Digital Asset Derivatives Exchange
supporting_sources.supports: The formal CFTC allegations against Binance and related entities.

timeline.description: A federal court entered an order concluding the CFTC enforcement action. Binance was required to pay $2.7 billion, and the order permanently enjoined Binance from specified violations of the Commodity Exchange Act and related requirements.

supporting_sources.title: Federal Court Enters Order Against Binance and Former CEO, Zhao, Concluding CFTC Enforcement Action
supporting_sources.supports: The confirmed federal court order, $2.7 billion payment, and permanent injunctive relief.

matters.risk_calculation: 25 typology + 40 confirmed stage + 0 direct attribution + 0 recency = 65.
matters.key_evidence: The CFTC reported both the original enforcement action and the subsequent federal court order imposing a $2.7 billion payment and permanent injunction.
matters.key_facts: The matter began as a formal civil enforcement action on March 27, 2023 and concluded with a federal court order on December 18, 2023. The formal allegations should not be described as independently confirmed misconduct beyond the confirmed court resolution.

supporting_sources.title: CFTC Charges Binance and Its Founder, Changpeng Zhao, with Willful Evasion of Federal Law and Operating an Illegal Digital Asset Derivatives Exchange
Federal Court Enters Order Against Binance and Former CEO, Zhao, Concluding CFTC Enforcement Action
supporting_sources.supports: The confirmed resolution, monetary payment, and injunction.

matters.matter_summary: The SEC filed a civil enforcement action against Binance entities and Changpeng Zhao alleging unregistered securities-related activities, misrepresentation of trading controls, and other securities-law violations. Emergency relief was granted in 2023, but the SEC later dismissed the civil enforcement action with prejudice on May 29, 2025.

event_geography.jurisdiction_or_authority: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission; U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia

timeline.description: The SEC filed 13 charges against Binance entities and Changpeng Zhao, including allegations concerning unregistered exchanges, broker-dealers, and clearing agencies; misrepresentation of trading controls and oversight on Binance.US; and unregistered offers and sales of securities.

supporting_sources.title: SEC Files 13 Charges Against Binance Entities and Founder Changpeng Zhao

timeline.description: The SEC obtained emergency relief requiring defendants to repatriate to the United States assets held for Binance.US customers and to maintain control of those assets in the United States.

supporting_sources.title: SEC Secures Emergency Relief to Protect Binance.US Customers’ Assets
supporting_sources.supports: The emergency relief and asset-control requirements.

timeline.description: The SEC stated that it filed a joint stipulation with Binance entities and Changpeng Zhao to dismiss with prejudice the civil enforcement action filed in June 2023.

supporting_sources.title: SEC Announces Dismissal of Civil Enforcement Action Against Binance Entities and Founder Changpeng Zhao
supporting_sources.supports: The favorable procedural resolution dismissing the civil enforcement action with prejudice.

matters.risk_calculation: CLEARED matter: final score is 0 under the required formula.
matters.key_evidence: The SEC reported formal charges and emergency relief in 2023, followed by dismissal with prejudice of the civil enforcement action on May 29, 2025.
matters.key_facts: The matter concerned Binance entities and related U.S. entities. The later dismissal is a favorable resolution and the earlier allegations must not be described as confirmed misconduct.

supporting_sources.title: SEC Files 13 Charges Against Binance Entities and Founder Changpeng Zhao
SEC Secures Emergency Relief to Protect Binance.US Customers’ Assets
supporting_sources.supports: The emergency relief issued during the litigation.

supporting_sources.title: SEC Announces Dismissal of Civil Enforcement Action Against Binance Entities and Founder Changpeng Zhao

excluded_matters.reason: No AFC wrongdoing; the article described supervisory requirements imposed on Binance Markets Limited rather than a specified AFC typology.

plain_english_summary.subject_profile_summary: Binance is a global cryptocurrency and blockchain-services brand operating through multiple jurisdiction-specific entities. Its services include digital-asset exchange, custody, payments, staking, institutional accounts, research, education, wallet, NFT, and other Web3 activities.
plain_english_summary.geography_summary: The focal geography is multinational. The supplied profile identifies operations or regulated entities in Abu Dhabi Global Market, France, Italy, Spain, Poland, Sweden, and Mexico. The adverse matters occurred primarily in the United States, with one excluded supervisory matter in the United Kingdom.
plain_english_summary.overall_risk_assessment: The calculated subject risk score is 85, Critical. The score is driven primarily by the confirmed U.S. Department of Justice criminal resolution involving Binance Holdings Limited and the confirmed CFTC enforcement resolution. The SEC matter is currently classified as CLEARED and contributes zero matter-risk points.
plain_english_summary.timeline_summary: The principal timeline runs from the CFTC formal action on March 27, 2023, through SEC formal charges and emergency relief in June 2023, the DOJ guilty plea on November 21, 2023, the CFTC court resolution on December 18, 2023, and the SEC dismissal with prejudice on May 29, 2025.
plain_english_summary.main_risk_drivers: The main risk drivers are the confirmed Bank Secrecy Act, unlicensed money-transmission, and IEEPA-related guilty plea and the confirmed CFTC resolution involving a $2.7 billion payment and permanent injunction. These matters were attributed directly to Binance entities identified in the source material.
plain_english_summary.confidence_note: Evidence confidence is High for the principal matters because the sources are official U.S. government and regulatory publications. Confidence is separate from risk severity and was not used in the score calculation.

limitations: The input identifies a brand and global business group rather than a uniquely specified legal entity.

Binance operates through multiple jurisdiction-specific companies; the cited proceedings may concern Binance Holdings Limited, Binance-affiliated entities, Binance Markets Limited, Binance.US entities, or other related parties rather than every entity using the Binance name.
The SEC civil enforcement action was dismissed with prejudice on May 29, 2025; the earlier SEC allegations are included with that subsequent procedural development.
The FCA action concerned Binance Markets Limited and was excluded because the supplied article described supervisory restrictions rather than AFC wrongdoing.
No single group-wide parent company, incorporation jurisdiction, registered address, or ultimate beneficial ownership structure was established from the supplied profile sources.
The output does not make an onboarding, offboarding, guilt, SAR-filing, or final compliance decision.

review_reasons: The subject is a global brand and business group with multiple legal entities, so entity-level attribution should be verified.
The DOJ and CFTC matters are material confirmed matters requiring review of the specific legal entities, products, jurisdictions, and applicable restrictions.
The SEC matter was formally alleged and later dismissed with prejudice; reviewers should distinguish the historical allegations from the favorable resolution.
The typology mapping is constrained by the specified taxonomy and should be independently reviewed for the intended compliance use.

subject_reports.final_conclusion: Binance is assessed at Critical subject-level risk with a score of 85. The result is driven by two independent confirmed matters: the 2023 U.S. Department of Justice criminal resolution involving Bank Secrecy Act, unlicensed money-transmission, and IEEPA-related violations, and the 2023 CFTC enforcement resolution involving a $2.7 billion payment and permanent injunction. The SEC civil enforcement matter is classified as CLEARED following dismissal with prejudice on May 29, 2025 and contributes no matter-risk points. Human review is required, particularly to confirm the relevant Binance legal entity and the applicability of each matter to the intended relationship or jurisdiction.

qa_warnings: The supplied assessment date was not explicitly provided; 2026-01-01 was retained from the supplied source retrieval dates for report completion.
The Binance brand and group do not identify one unique legal entity. Matter attribution is therefore entity-specific and should not automatically be extended to every Binance-affiliated entity.
The supplied profile contains an individual_profile object even though subject_type is Entity; it was preserved as received but not used as the final profile type.
The supplied source data describes the SEC matter as dismissed with prejudice on May 29, 2025; this was treated as a CLEARED favorable resolution and not as confirmed misconduct.
No independent duplicate matter was created for related articles describing the same SEC, CFTC, or DOJ proceedings.
